subject: DevOps
title: Incident Response Plans
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Incident Response Plans.

hook.text: The breach hit Friday night. Nobody knew who to call or what to do. Chaos doubled the damage.

buildup.text: An incident response plan defines roles, channels, and steps before trouble arrives.

discovery.text: Key phases: detect, contain, eradicate, recover, learn. Each has specific actions and owners.

twist.text: Plans must be practiced. Tabletop exercises simulate breaches and reveal gaps before real ones hit.

climax.text: Blameless postmortems capture lessons. They improve the plan and prevent the same mistake twice.

punchline.text: Plan before the incident. Practice the plan. Improve after.

quiz.question: Why should incident response plans be practiced through tabletop exercises?
options: Practice reveals gaps and ensures the team knows their roles before a real incident
Tabletop exercises are required by all compliance frameworks
Practicing makes incidents less likely to occur
